reviews.review_description:
Medic Simulator: Combat Zone puts you in the role of a combat medic in the middle of a war zone, where your job is to save wounded soldiers under intense pressure.

Sometimes injured troops are brought to you by ambulance, but other times you must head straight into the battlefield yourself to rescue them. Once back at the military hospital, the real challenge begins: extracting bullets and foreign objects, stabilizing wounds, sawing damaged limbs, and applying bandages to keep your patients alive.

If that wasn’t stressful enough, you also need to stay alert for guerrilla soldiers who may attack while you’re operating. This constant tension adds an extra layer of excitement to the gameplay.

Overall, it’s a solid and engaging experience with good variety in missions and mechanics. A fun and well-executed VR title that keeps you on your toes. 💉🎮

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the awesome review! Really glad you enjoyed the game and had fun with the experience. Your feedback means a lot to us!

reviews.review_description:
Jumped in, not knowing what to expect, pleasantly surprised, would love some smooth turning please and a little sharper graphics.
The medical side works well, cutting open the chest is quite satisfying, as is using the tweezers to remove shrapnel.
Sprays work really well, the added gun fight as you try to heal your patient adds a little tension.
For early access it’s not too bad.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your feedback! We’re glad you enjoyed the medical mechanics.
We’ll work on adding smooth turning and improving the graphics in upcoming updates.

reviews.review_description:
You drop right into the action, it does not need a tutorial as it explains as you go. I only dropped into it long enough to save some lives and get my name on the leader board.
The graphics are ok, but the AI on the enemy that you come across both in the op theatre and in the field is not the best and seem to wait around to get shot, which is helpful.  The casualty’s injuries get more complex as you progress which is great as you learn how to use the equipment and I was doing my first let amputation before I met my end by getting shot.  There is a General in the room with you barking orders but don’t expect help off him whilst you deal with patients and shooting enemies breaking in lol…
There are some setting tweaks regarding movement and sound but I would love to see a setting for seated play which is my preferred option, if and when that gets added I will up my review to 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback. I agree — the enemy AI is currently being improved. In this early stage of the project we focused more on the surgical mechanics, so the enemies were intentionally made easier to deal with to avoid making the experience too difficult for the player.

I also appreciate you mentioning the option to play seated. This is actually the second review pointing that out, and I really appreciate you highlighting it.

We’ll take a close look at this and work on improving support for seated play in a future update.

reviews.review_description:
I think the game is really good, but I would like it to be a bit more realistic, especially on the battlefield with things like applying adrenaline and morphine.

I’ve only just started playing, but I’m excited to explore more!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! We’ll be bringing new updates to make the game even more fun.
